Format: Use bullet points, avoid markdown.
Present findings in a single narrative, integrating Part 1 and Part 2.

The investigation into tensions between anachronism risks and Whiggish narratives within Pre-Kantian rationalism and empiricism—set against the historical context of Washington (population 7,705,281, presumably referencing Washington State or the broader Pacific Northwest region in the early 21st century)—reveals methodological and interpretive challenges rooted in how historians of philosophy engage with early modern thought.
